channels_scanned.note: SKILL.md default-seed channel_id was stale (resolved to Digital Trends) — corrected this run

tests.why: All 3 top-3 competitors run 25-90min+ (Fern ~30min stable, MrBallen ~31min stable, LEMMiNO trending to 60-90min+ flagships). Our long-forms (11:46, 13:12, 11:54, 11:09) are 2-3x shorter than the niche floor. Not proposing we jump straight to 30min+ with a sub-1k catalogue, but the gap is wide enough to warrant a controlled step up.

tests.what: Reinforce non-question, non-date-dump title format across next batch
tests.why: 0% of 24 sampled titles across all 3 competitors are questions or contain a date/year — pure declarative statements ('How to Escape Auschwitz', 'She had a DEADLY sixth sense', 'The Enduring Mystery of Jack the Ripper'). This directly informs the pending EXP-TITLE-001 (numbers_iconic_contradiction vs iconic_question_year) — competitor evidence leans toward the non-question variant, but the experiment should still run to completion on our own data before we lock it in.

adoptions.what: Punchy declarative titles (no question marks, no date/year dumps), avg 5-8 words
adoptions.why: Confirmed on 3/3 top channels this scan (LEMMiNO avg 4.9 words, Fern avg 5.5, MrBallen avg 8.5 — all 0% questions, 0% dates). Reinforces our own KNOWN_BAD 'date dumps' rule with fresh cross-channel evidence.

adoptions.what: Consistent dark/desaturated thumbnail base with exactly one dominant saturated accent, no clutter
adoptions.why: Confirmed on 3/3 channels (LEMMiNO single-hue vector, Fern dark+bold text+red/orange accent, MrBallen dark+single color-graded photo+caption) — matches our own locked 'ONE dominant element' KNOWN_BAD rule, reinforcing it rather than changing anything.

avoidances.what: Treating comment-reply silence as evidence that active community replies don't matter
avoidances.why: All 3 top channels (11M, 5M, 8.9M+ subs) run a near-zero comment-reply engagement model and still thrive — but that's survivorship data from channels that were never small, not proof the tactic is neutral for a cold-start channel. Don't use this scan to justify dropping our own active-reply strategy (skills/community-manager/SKILL.md) — see blindspots below.

content_gaps.hook: Cover the Zodiac Killer investigation from the press/police angle (not the murders themselves)
content_gaps.frequency: explicit ask on LEMMiNO's Kennedy Assassination video, 3.5k-like comment

content_gaps.hook: A 'viewer context follow-up' short/community post surfacing amateur theories + cultural context viewers volunteer unprompted in comments
content_gaps.frequency: seen on 4/4 sampled MrBallen videos — a pattern MrBallen itself never acts on

experiments_to_open.hypothesis: 16-18min long-form beats current ~12min baseline on average-view-duration and session-time contribution, without hurting completion rate
Removing the red arrow-pointer annotation from thumbnails (letting the AI base photo + headline text alone carry it) performs at parity or better on CTR
experiments_to_open.baseline: current locked thumb recipe includes a red #D9343F arrow polygon pointing at the iconic detail

blindspots: Experiment cap already oversubscribed: 4 experiments are currently 'pending' (EXP-HOOK-001, EXP-TITLE-001, EXP-VOICE-001, PASSIVE-TIME-001) against a hard cap of 3 — both experiments_to_open above will be auto-skipped by cross_reference.py apply until an existing one closes (needs 14+ day age + min impressions). Next session should prioritize running experiment_tracker.py check to see if any can close.

No causal read on whether active comment-reply engagement drives sub growth for a cold-start channel — all 3 scanned competitors are large, established channels with a no-reply model; that's not comparable evidence for a sub-1k channel building initial trust.
No data on poll usage/effectiveness in this niche — all 3 channels' Community post UI renders the poll wrapper but none actually ran a multiple-choice poll this cycle.

Co-watch/sidebar signal (Phase 2) is still unreliable at our current view counts (1-115 views/video) — logged separately in niche_intel/competitors.json with a watchlist of small dubbed-mystery channels to revisit once our catalogue has more traction.

Fern's likes/comments counts for videos 6-8 (of 8) were not collected (estimated publish dates only) to stay within the per-channel navigation budget — minor gap in the duration/cadence dataset, not expected to change conclusions.
